This is my first move as Player RED, starting with all the resources (100 Dollars). Since Player BLUE has no Dollars to trade and no prior interaction, I must propose an offer that incentivizes BLUE to accept while also ensuring I maximize my own outcome. Given there are no future games, BLUE has no reason to reject in hopes of a better deal later—this is a one-shot interaction.

A rational BLUE would reject any offer where they receive 0 Dollars, as the game ends with no resources if rejected. However, since BLUE has nothing to trade, I can only offer them a "share" of the 100 Dollars in exchange for nothing (since BLUE has no resources to give). But offering them anything would mean I give up my own resources for no gain.

Wait—this seems like a dilemma. If I propose a trade where I give BLUE some Dollars (e.g., 1 Dollar) for nothing, BLUE would rationally reject because they get nothing in return, and the game ends with both losing everything (which is worse than accepting even a tiny amount). But if I propose 0 Dollars, BLUE has no incentive to accept because they gain nothing.

This suggests that under these rules, BLUE would only accept if I offer them something (even if it's irrational or symbolic), because rejecting guarantees a worse outcome (both lose everything). But since BLUE has no resources to trade, the only way to make BLUE accept is to offer them something, even if it's just a tiny fraction, to avoid the "reject and lose everything" outcome.

Alternatively, perhaps BLUE would accept even a 0-Dollar offer because rejecting would mean both lose everything, but accepting 0 is better than losing 100 (though that seems counterintuitive because they gain nothing). However, standard game theory suggests that BLUE would reject 0 because it's equivalent to both losing 100.

Given this, I should propose a trade where BLUE gets at least 1 Dollar, even though BLUE has nothing to give in return. This ensures BLUE accepts (because rejecting would mean losing everything, while accepting gives them 1 Dollar). The best offer for me would be to give the minimal amount possible (1 Dollar) to secure the rest (99 Dollars), as BLUE has no leverage.

If I offer 0 Dollars, BLUE may reject (since they gain nothing, and rejecting would mean both lose 100, which is worse than gaining 0 but not losing anything). But if I offer 1 Dollar, BLUE accepts because they gain 1 Dollar instead of losing 100.

Therefore, I propose giving BLUE 1 Dollar for nothing, ensuring they accept and I keep 99 Dollars.
</reason>
